internal_services.steps: ["End-user submits the approved and funded PR along with PPMP and other supporting documents.","Receive and record the PR; check completeness, correctness, and appropriateness of supporting documents; assign control number.","Determine the appropriate method of procurement: Competitive/Public Bidding, Direct Contracting, Alternative Mode of Procurement (AMP SVP/Shopping), or Common-use Supplies at PS-DBM.","If Competitive Bidding: Conduct Pre-Procurement Conference with BAC Technical Working Group to prepare bidding documents.","If Competitive Bidding: Prepare, finalize, and approve Invitation to Bid (IB) and Bidding Documents; post on PhilGEPS, DILG website, and conspicuous places.","If Competitive Bidding: Prospective suppliers attend Pre-Bidding Conference to clarify requirements, terms, and technical specifications.","If Competitive Bidding: Suppliers submit bids on specified date and time; conduct Opening of Bids and check documents.","If Competitive Bidding: Conduct detailed evaluation of all eligible bids; declare Single/Lowest Calculated Bidder (S/LCB) as Evaluated.","If Competitive Bidding: Evaluated S/LCB submits Post-Qualification documents; verify and validate all statements and documents.","Issue Notice of Award (NOA) to Single/Lowest Calculated Responsive Bidder (S/LCRB); receive performance security.","Prepare and issue Purchase Order (PO) or Contract to S/LCRB; furnish COA with certified copies within 5 days.","Prepare and serve Notice to Proceed (NTP); post signed NTP and PO/contract on PhilGEPS and DILG websites.","Supplier/service provider delivers goods/services and submits Delivery Receipt/Sales Invoice.","Receive delivery; prepare Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR); conduct Technical Property Inspection.","Verify items/services as to quantity and conformity with specifications; inspect and accept deliveries.","Store delivered items; record and update stock card; assign and tag property numbers for PPE/semi-expendable items.","Furnish COA with copies of delivery documents within 24 hours from acceptance; submit to Accounting Division for journal entry.","End-user submits Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) upon receipt of delivered items.","Prepare Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) or Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS); issue deliveries to requesting office.","Update stock card of issued items; administer Client Satisfaction Survey (CSS); report CSS results.","Prepare Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) and Report of Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RSPI); forward IAR with supporting documents for payment processing."]

internal_services.label: Processing and Payment of Claims in the DILG Central Office
internal_services.description: Facilitates processing and payment of various claims (salary, allowances, travel, procurement, etc.) through LDDAP-ADA or check issuance.
internal_services.whoMayAvail: DILG officials/employees, financial institutions, NGAs, LGUs, SUCs, CSOs, suppliers, and other claimants
internal_services.requirements: [{"text":"ORS (Obligation Request and Status) - one original and three photocopies","where":"COA website / Government Accounting Manual Vol. 2"},{"text":"DV (Disbursement Voucher) - one original and one photocopy","where":"COA website / Government Accounting Manual Vol. 2"},{"text":"Claim-type specific supporting documents (salary, allowances, travel, procurement, etc.)","where":"Various sources per claim type"}]
internal_services.steps: ["Submit copy of ORS/BURS and DV with corresponding documentary requirements depending on claim type.","Receive and check the documents (1 hour).","Review and process ORS/BURS; allocate funds, verify availability, assign UACS Object Codes (2 hours).","Validate ORS/BURS correctness and accuracy; initially approve in Financial Reporting System (FRS) (1 hour).","Certify availability of allotment and obligation; approve ORS/BURS and sign physical copy (1 hour).","Forward approved ORS/BURS to Accounting Division or Procurement Management Division (50 minutes).","Receive DV with supporting documents; check against DMS details; generate DV number from FRS (30 minutes).","Process DV: check completeness and compliance with audit/accounting rules (2 working days).","Record claim details on Individual Index of Payment Card; update FRS details (2 hours each).","Verify availability of cash (30 minutes).","Review claim for completeness of supporting documents, accuracy of computation, and correctness of entries (1 hour).","Release approved DV to AS-GSD Cash Section or approving officials (30 minutes).","Receive and determine payment method: LDDAP-ADA (bank credit) or check.","For LDDAP-ADA: prepare and sign LDDAP-ADA for issuance (2.5 hours total).","For check: prepare check and record in check register (30 minutes).","Check accuracy of entries; route to designated signatories for approval (7 hours).","Prepare LDDAP-Advice of Checks Issued and Cancelled (ACIC) and Financial Data Entry System (FinDES) (2 hours).","Route LDDAP-ACIC and FinDES to authorized signatories (30 minutes).","Receive payment through LDDAP-ADA bank credit to account or through check and accomplish Client Satisfaction Survey (CSS) (1 hour).","Transmit approved documents to Government Servicing Bank (LBP) for crediting or release check to client."]

Reimbursement of Meals; Catering/Lease/Accommodation; PS-DBM Supplies; Mobilization Fee; Remittances; Fidelity Bond; Lease Purchase; Subscriptions; E-CLIP Assistance for Former Rebels; SGLG Incentive; Donations; Internet/Communication; Checkbooks/Accountable Forms; Training Fees; and other similar claims","where":"Varies by claim type – refer to specific requirements per category (COA, PSA, BIR, AS-PD, FMS, GSD/GSS, PMD, Banks, Suppliers, Agencies concerned, etc.)"}]
internal_services.steps: ["Submit the copy of ORS/BURS and DV with corresponding documentary requirements depending on the type of claim to FAD – Budget Section (BS).","FAD – BS Administrative Aide/Assistant/Officer receives and checks the documents (1 hour).","FAD – BS Administrative Officer reviews and processes the ORS/BURS, checks completeness of supporting documents, allocates funds by verifying against availability and assigns UACS Object Code, approves and signs Box B of the ORS/BURS to certify that allotment is available and the claim is obligated for its deemed purpose (2 hours).","FAD – BS Administrative Officer validates the correctness and accuracy of the processed ORS/BURS, initially approves the ORS/BURS in the FRS, and affixes initial in Box B of the ORS/BURS physical copy (1 hour).","FAD – BS Administrative Officer certifies the availability of allotment and obligation, approves the ORS/BURS in the FRS, and signs Box B of the ORS/BURS physical copy (50 minutes).","FAD – BS Administrative Assistant/Aide/Officer detaches the original signed copy of the ORS/BURS for filing, updates monitoring log sheet, and forwards the approved ORS/BURS to FAD – Accounting Section (AS) or Supply and General Services Section (SGSS) (40 minutes).","FAD – AS Administrative Aide/Assistant/Officer receives the DV with the supporting documents, checks against the details in the Document Management System (DMS) and monitoring log sheet, generates DV number from the FRS, and assigns processor (30 minutes).","FAD – AS Administrative Aide/Assistant/Accountant processes DV by checking the completeness and appropriateness of supporting documents and its compliance with auditing and accounting rules and regulations (2 working days); records the details of the claim on the Individual Index of Payment (IIP) Card and updates the needed details in the Financial Reporting System (FRS) (4 hours).","FAD – AS Accountant verifies the availability of cash (30 minutes).","FAD – AS Accountant reviews the claim as to the completeness of the supporting documents, accuracy in the computation of the claim, correctness in the entries of Box B and IIP Card, as well as the details in the FRS (1 hour).","FAD – AS Administrative Aide/Assistant forwards to approving authority (15 minutes).","Division Chief, FAD / Assistant Regional Director / Regional Director signs Box B of the ORS/BURS (30 minutes).","FAD – AS Administrative Aide releases/routes approved DV to FAD – Cash Section (15 minutes).","FAD – Cash Section Administrative Aide/Assistant/Officer receives, records, and checks the approved/signed DV with attached supporting documents and determines whether payment will be through LDDAP-ADA or check (1 hour).","FAD – Cash Section Administrative Assistant/Officer prepares LDDAP-ADA (2 hours) or, if check, prepares the check and records in the check register (30 minutes).","FAD – Cash Section Administrative Assistant/Officer reviews and signs LDDAP-ADA, and verifies completeness of signature and checks the amount against the DV for issuance of LDDAP-ADA (30 minutes).","FAD – AS Accountant / Head of Office or authorized representative checks the accuracy of entries in LDDAP-ADA and affixes initials (7 hours); FAD – Cash Section Administrative Assistant/Officer routes all reviewed original LDDAP-ADAs and checks with supporting documents to designated signatories (30 minutes).","FAD – Cash Section Administrative Assistant/Officer prepares LDDAP – Advice of Checks Issued and Cancelled (ACIC) and Financial Data Entry System (FinDES) (2 hours).","Administrative Assistant/Officer/Accountant/Supervising Administrative Officer/Division Chief, FAD/Assistant Regional Director/Regional Director reviews and signs the LDDAP-ACIC and FinDES (30 minutes).","Client receives payment through direct credit to payee's bank account or through check and accomplishes the Client Satisfaction Survey (CSS). internal_services.steps: ["Submit the copy of DV with corresponding documentary requirements depending on the type of claim to FAS.","FAS Administrative Aide/Assistant receives and checks the documents (1 hour).","FAS Administrative Aide/Assistant processes DV by checking the completeness and appropriateness of supporting documents and its compliance with auditing and accounting rules and regulations, and records the details of the claim on the Individual Index of Payment (IIP) Card (2 working days).","FAS Administrative Aide/Assistant verifies the availability of cash (30 minutes).","FAS Administrative Aide/Assistant reviews and approves the processed DV through reviewing the completeness and appropriateness of the supporting documents and its compliance with accounting and auditing rules and regulations and accuracy in the computation of the claim (1 hour).","FAS Administrative Aide/Assistant releases approved DV to approving officials (30 minutes).","FAS Administrative Aide/Assistant receives, records, and checks the approved/signed DV with attached supporting documents and determines whether payment will be through LDDAP-ADA or check (1 hour).","FAS Administrative Aide/Assistant reviews and verifies the completeness of signature on the DV and reviews the amount of the check against the DVs (30 minutes).","FAS Administrative Assistant prepares the check and records in the check register (40 minutes).","FAS Administrative Assistant reviews and signs the check (7 hours and 30 minutes).","FAS Administrative Assistant routes all reviewed checks with supporting documents to designated signatories (30 minutes); prepares LDDAP – Advice of Checks Issued and Cancelled (ACIC) and routes the LDDAP-ACIC to authorized signatories (20 minutes).","Provincial Director signs the LDDAP-ACIC (30 minutes).","Client receives payment through check and accomplishes the Client Satisfaction Survey (CSS). FAS Administrative Aide/Assistant transmits approved LDDAP-ACIC to the Government Servicing Bank (LBP) for crediting to payee's/claimant's bank account or releases check to client and administers CSS (1 hour)."]

internal_services.label: Transfer of Funds
internal_services.description: Facilitates the issuance of Sub-Allotment Release Order (SRO) and Notice of Transfer Allocation (NTA) to DILG Regional Offices for various programs, activities, and projects requiring fund transfers.
internal_services.whoMayAvail: DILG Regional Offices/Operating Units
internal_services.requirements: [{"text":"One (1) original copy of Letter Request for Fund Transfer (SRO and NTA) with any of the following applicable attachments: One (1) photocopy of Operations Plan and Budget (OPB), One (1) photocopy of Activity Design, One (1) copy of funding requirement breakdown per Program, Activity, and Project (PAP) with corresponding expense account name/code, One (1) photocopy of Notice of Fund Transfer (if not included in OPB), One (1) copy of NTA schedule for download","where":"Requesting DILG Regional Office/Operating Unit"},{"text":"For Barangay Officials' Death and Burial Assistance (BODBA) and Administrative Personnel Benefits (APB) claims, one (1) photocopy of Special Allotment Release Order (SARO) and Notice of Cash Allocation (NCA) with the list of beneficiaries","where":"Department of Budget and Management (DBM)"}]
internal_services.steps: ["Submit the requirements to FMS – Budget Division (BD).","FMS – BD Administrative Assistant checks, receives, and records the fund transfer request in the Process Summary Log Sheet (PSL) based on the set documentary requirements and forwards to FMS – BD Chief. For Request for Fund Transfer, receives the request through the Document Management System (DMS) or physical copy with applicable supporting documents. For SARO with NCA, receives and records the copy of SARO with NCA from DBM from the Office of FMS Director with complete supporting documents (1 hour).","FMS – BD Division Chief reviews the fund transfer request for eligibility and provides instructions (1 hour).","FMS – BD Administrative Officer checks the details of the fund request, including the amount and source of funds, against the provided supporting documents, and prepares and attaches sub-allotment breakdown and transmittal memorandum (1 working day and 4 hours).","FMS – BD Administrative Officer reviews the fund transfer request and accompanying memorandum. If the request and supporting documents are complete and accurate, affixes signature in the sub-allotment breakdown; otherwise, returns to the operating unit or processor (4 hours).","FMS – BD Supervising Administrative Officer conducts final review on the fund transfer request. If found in order, prepares the SRO and forwards to FMS – BD Chief (2 hours).","FMS – BD Chief certifies the availability of funds and affixes initials to the SRO and transmittal memorandum (30 minutes).","FMS – BD Administrative Assistant/Officer updates the PSL and forwards the SRO to the Office of FMS Director to obtain his/her signature on the Certification of Funds Availability section of the SRO (30 minutes).","Director, FMS / Assistant Secretary for Administration, Finance, and Comptrollership (ASAFC) / Authorized Signatories approve the SRO. Note: The approval of SRO depends on the availability of authorized signatories (1 hour).","FMS – BD Administrative Assistant/Officer/Designated Budget Staff receives the approved SRO and updates PSL (1 working day); scans and uploads the approved SRO and supporting documents to the Financial Reporting System (FRS) and notifies the concerned FMS – Accounting Division (AD) staff to prepare the NTA; administers the Client Satisfaction Survey (CSS) to the requesting unit.","FMS – AD Administrative Assistant obtains the approved SRO from the FRS and prepares NTA (will be prepared for the month specified in the request and downloaded to the DILG Regional Offices based on the attached schedule). Note: If the SRO is approved on the last week of a quarter, NTA will be prepared on the following month's first (1st) working day (1 working day).","FMS – AD Chief reviews the NTA and affixes initials (1 hour and 30 minutes).","FMS – AD Administrative Assistant forwards the NTA to the Office of FMS Director and Office of ASAFC for signature/approval (30 minutes).","Director, FMS/ASAFC approves the NTA and forwards it to FMS – AD for information and recording (2 hours). Office of ASAFC Administrative Assistant handles routing.","Client receives NTA. FMS – AD Administrative Assistant submits the approved NTA to Landbank to effect the crediting of funds to the concerned DILG Regional Office's LBP – MDS account (2 hours)."]

internal_services.label: Provision of Internal Audit Services
internal_services.description: Delivers the main function of the Internal Audit Service (IAS) through the conduct of Compliance, Management, and Operations Audits to determine compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, including Department issuances and to evaluate the adequacy of internal controls and the 4Es (Effectiveness, Efficiency, Economical, and Ethical) of operations. This service includes the four (4) phases of the audit process: Audit Engagement Planning, Audit Execution, Audit Reporting, and Audit Follow-up.
internal_services.whoMayAvail: SILG, DILG Top Officials; Auditees: LG Sector (DILG Central Office - Offices, Bureaus, and Services; DILG Regional and Field Offices; Local Government Academy), Interior Sector (National Headquarters and Regional and Field Offices of Bureau of Fire Protection and Bureau of Jail Management and Penology; Philippine Public Safety College), Other Attached Agencies (National Youth Commission; National Commission on Muslim Filipinos; Philippine Commission on Women)
internal_services.requirements: [{"text":"Physical or electronic copy of the documents, records, and reports relevant to the audit area/coverage","where":"Auditee"}]
internal_services.steps: ["SILG issues a Department Order (DO) directing the conduct of audit. IAS Audit Team Leader and Members prepare and review the DO with transmittal memorandum on the conduct of internal audit for SILG's approval (2 working days).","Director, IAS reviews and approves the transmittal memorandum and forwards to the action officer for release (1 working day).","IAS Administrative Staff releases the DO to the Office of the Secretary (OSEC) for SILG's approval/signature (1 hour).","Auditee provides relevant documents, records, and reports of the audit areas. IAS Audit Team Leader and Members prepare and review Audit Notification Memorandum (ANM) on the conduct of the internal audit and matrix or list of required documents (2 working days).","Director, IAS reviews and signs the ANM and forwards to the action officer for release (1 working day).","IAS Administrative Staff releases the ANM with the attached approved DO and matrix or list of required documents through the Document Management System (DMS) or Central Records Section for distribution (1 hour).","AUDIT ENGAGEMENT PLANNING: Auditee provides relevant documents, records, and reports of the audit areas. Audit Teams gather initial documents through research and receipt of relevant documents, records, and reports (1 working day); process gathered documents through table review/validation and interview (2 working days); request for an orientation meeting with the concerned office/personnel, if necessary (1 working day); process updated documents gathered from the auditees and review relevance and completeness of information in the Audit Area Profile (1 working day).","Audit Team Members prepare and review the Audit Engagement Plan (2 working days).","Director, IAS reviews and approves the Audit Engagement Plan (1 working day).","Audit Team Leader and Members prepare and review the Audit Work Program; indicate detailed audit work instructions to accomplish the steps and methodologies; and prepare the Summary of Audit Team Roles and Responsibilities as attachment (2 working days).","Director, IAS approves the Audit Work Program (1 working day).","Audit Team Leader and Members, and Director, IAS identify and list the specific laws/policies/guidelines/standards and indicate the requirements and sample MOVs for review and approval (1 working day).","Audit Team Leader and Members prepare the System/Process Flowcharts from the analyzed process/system documentation to identify controls and other process details (2 working days); refer to the audit checklists to identify controls from CC and ICC, and input in the Walkthrough Test for review by the Audit Team Leader (2 working days).","AUDIT EXECUTION: Auditee participates in the Entry Conference and provides reply or comments/concurs with the Minutes of the Entry Conference. Top Officials receive memorandum invitations for the conduct of entry conference to deliver message. Audit Team Leader and Members prepare and review the Notice of Entry Conference Memorandum (NECM) to all concerned offices; review and sign the NECM; and forward and release the NECM to concerned offices (6 hours).","Audit Team Leader and Members prepare memorandum invitations for Top Officials for review by the Audit Team Leader and signature of IAS Director; Director, IAS and IAS Administrative Staff forward/release the memorandum invitations for Top Officials and concerned offices (6 hours).","Audit Team Leader and Members, and Director, IAS prepare Entry Conference presentations; review and approve the presentations (6 hours).","Team Leader/Audit Team conducts Entry Conference with the Auditees (2 hours).","Audit Team Members take down notes including the Auditee's view of the overall framework for the conduct of the audit and record proceedings of the meeting (2 hours).","Audit Team Leader and Members, Director, IAS, and IAS Administrative Staff prepare the Entry Conference Notes for review (Sub-level Entry Conference) or IAS Director (General Entry Conference) and release with transmittal memorandum for concurrence of the Auditee (4 hours).","Auditee participates in the actual conduct of audit by providing requested documents during the audit, submits self for checklist administration; participates in the interview and walkthrough; provides logistical support, if necessary; and replies/comments on the Interim Audit Memorandum, if any. Audit Team Leader and Members process information documents in the checklists and determine answers with complete/lacking information/documents. Record the result in the corresponding checklist (2 hours); perform walkthrough of documents and process flow on the subject matter of audit, test of controls if present, record the result in the Walkthrough Test (2 hours); perform test of control of document and process flow based on sampling, conduct substantive tests through samples and record the result in the Test of Control, include all non-existing controls in the Summary of Gaps (2 hours); determine if interview is needed, conduct the interview and document the result in the Interview Notes (2 hours).","If there are additional documents needed and/or clarification, Audit Team Leader and Members prepare Audit Inquiry Memorandum (AIM), review and sign AIM, release AIM to concerned auditees (1 working day).","Audit Team Leader and Members draft audit findings in terms of the 4Cs, prepare the Individual Audit Findings (1 working day); draft audit condition on the results of review and appraisal of control in case of Compliance and Management Audit based on sampling and evaluation of 4Es in case of Operations Audit; process probable/root cause analyses on the established audit condition and draft the audit cause (Probable Cause for Compliance Audit and Root Cause for Management/Operations Audit); process conditions with criteria to draw audit conclusions.","Audit Team Leader and Members, and Director, IAS draft report on significant deficiencies requiring immediate action and its probable cause through the issuance of Interim Audit Memorandum, if any (1 working day).","Audit Team indexes, files, collects, and prepares workpapers for analysis and processes all workpapers for the preparation of audit findings (1 working day).","Audit Team Members, Team Leader/Director, IAS process the Individual Audit Findings and consolidate results in the Initial Audit Report. Draft the Highlights of Audit Findings in terms of 2Cs (criteria and condition). Prepare the transmittal memorandum for review (Sub-level Exit Conference) or IAS Director (General Exit Conference) (1 working day).","Auditee participates in the actual conduct of audit; receives the Notice of Exit Conference Memorandum and communicates with the Audit Team. Top Officials receive and confirm attendance/participation in the exit conference. Audit Team Members, Team Leader/Director, IAS prepare, review, and approve the Progress Assessment Report (PARe). Accomplish the Sections A, C, and D of PARe before the sub-level Exit Conference. Consolidate the results of Sections A, C, and D of PARe of Sub-level Exit Conference and accomplish Sections B and E before the General Exit Conference (1 working day).","Audit Team Leader and Members discuss necessary documents and activities in the conduct of exit conference for the identification of attendees, location, and schedule. Prepare and review the Notice of Exit Conference Memorandum (2 hours).","Audit Team Leader and Members, Director, IAS, and IAS Administrative Staff prepare and review the memorandum invitations for Top Officials. Review and sign the memorandum invitations for Top Officials. Forward/release the memorandum invitations for Top Officials and to concerned offices (2 hours).","Audit Team Leader and Members, and Director, IAS prepare the presentations. Review and approve the presentations using the Exit Conference presentation template (4 hours).","Auditees participate in the Exit Conference to provide initial comments on the Highlights of Audit Findings; Receive/accept the Highlights of Audit Findings and submit Management Reply/Comments. Audit Team Leader and Members conduct Exit Conference with the Head of Internal Audit and/or Audit Team Leader and the Auditee (3 hours); take down notes including additional information to be considered in the audit report and record proceedings of the meeting (3 hours).","Audit Team Leader and Members, and Director, IAS prepare the Exit Conference Notes, attach the Highlights of Audit Findings requiring submission of Management Comments within 10 working days and transmittal memorandum for review (Sub-level Exit Conference) or IAS Director (General Exit Conference) (1 working day).","Audit Teams administer the Audit Feedback Survey Form to the attendees of the exit conference and to other key auditees. Record and analyze the result in the CSS Summary Log Sheet and in the Internal Audit Feedback Survey Analysis Report. Process and include the results in the Monitoring and Feedback section of the Internal Audit Report (10 minutes).","AUDIT REPORTING: SILG approves the Internal Audit Report. Audit Team Members refer to the Test of Control workpapers, analyze results, and summarize the deviations noted from the conduct of the test of controls in the Summary of Gaps (4 working days).","Audit Team Leaders and Members refer to the Sub-level IARs and prepare IAR (24 working days).","Audit Team Leader and Members, and Director, IAS prepare transmittal memorandum for SILG with memorandum to Auditees directing the implementation of the recommendations and requiring the submission of the auditee's action plan (1 working day).","IAS Administrative Staff releases the approved IAR with memorandum to Auditees directing the implementation of the recommendations and requiring the submission of the auditee's action plan (30 minutes).","AUDIT FOLLOW-UP: Auditee implements the Audit Recommendations and reports status of implementation through the submission of AAPIS to IAS and participates in the conduct of Audit Follow-up validation activity, as necessary; Receives Audit Inquiry Memorandum, communicates to concerned office/personnel and submits additional documents requested; Receives the IAsCARes. Audit Team Members process the Internal Audit Report and prepare the Audit Follow-up Program listing the audit activities to be performed for the evaluation of compliance with audit recommendations for review and approval of IAS Director (2 working days).","Audit Team Leader and Members, and Director, IAS prepare, review, and approve the Audit Follow-up Notification Memorandum (AFNM) with attached AAPIS. Release the AFNM to concerned offices (2 working days).","Audit Team Members process the submitted AAPIS to validate and evaluate the documents and supporting evidences submitted, and status of action or actions taken by the auditee on each recommendation using the IAsCARes.","Audit Team Leader/Members prepare Audit Inquiry Memorandum requesting additional documents needed and/or clarification in the implementation of audit recommendations. Record receipt of additional documents of implementation, perform cursory evaluation on the checklist of submitted documents and process the additional documents received (2 working days per quarter for 4 quarters).","Audit Team Leader/Members, Director, IAS, and IAS Administrative Staff prepare and review the IAsCARes and transmittal memorandum to communicate to the auditee the result of the monitoring and assessment of their compliance to audit recommendations and release to the concerned office (20 working days per quarter for 4 quarters).","SILG approves the Internal Audit Follow-up Report. After the fourth (4th) IAsCARes or validated full compliance, whichever comes early, Audit Team Leader and Members, and Director, IAS prepare and review the IAFR (30 working days).","IAS Administrative Staff submits the IAFR with transmittal memorandum to OSEC for SILG's approval.","Audit Teams, Team Leader, and Director, IAS prepare, review, and approve the Completion Assessment Report (1 working day).","IAS Administrative Staff retains documented information in accordance with the Control of Retained Documented Information Procedure and Master List of Retained Documented Information (30 minutes)."]

The end-user will request technical assistance and ISTMS/RICTU technical personnel will provide resolution or recommendation. Details will be documented in the ICT Technical Assistance Request Form (TARF).
internal_services.whoMayAvail: DILG Employees

ISTMS/RICTU Technical Personnel secures signature of the end-user in the ICT TARF and completely accomplishes the documentation. Note: No signature required for online ICT TARF (30 minutes)."]

The certification is issued if upon the verification in the Trial and Investigation Division (TID)'s official records/database, there is no pending administrative case/s filed before the Department against such personnel or LGU official.

internal_services.label: Issuance of Brand Identity Manual (BIM) Certification of Compliance

The service evaluates whether sample designs comply with the DILG's BIM standards and either approves the design or issues comments/recommendations for changes if the design does not comply.

internal_services.office: Public Affairs and Communication Service – Creative Services Division (PACS – CSD)

internal_services.whoMayAvail: All DILG – Central Office Operating Units
internal_services.office: Public Affairs and Communication Service – Creative Services Division (PACS – CSD)
internal_services.requirements: [{"text":"One (1) original or electronic copy of Job Request Form","where":"PACS – CSD"},{"text":"One (1) original or electronic copy of each of the supporting documents","where":"Client"}]
internal_services.steps: ["Submit the Job Request Form and supporting documents to PACS – CSD via the Document Management System (DMS) or e-mail.","Administrative Officer, PACS – CSD receives and records the Job Request (2 minutes).","Division Chief, PACS – CSD evaluates the request received (10 minutes).","Division Chief, PACS – CSD discusses the details of the request and conceptualizes the job request design (2 minutes).","Division Chief, PACS – CSD designates the job order to the Graphic Artist (1 minute).","Administrative Officer/Graphic Artist, PACS – CSD performs the graphics and layout design task (15 working days).","Administrative Officer/Graphic Artist, PACS – CSD submits the initial draft of the graphics and layout design task (5 minutes).","Division Chief, PACS – CSD reviews and approves the initial draft (5 minutes).","Administrative Officer, PACS – CSD releases the approved draft to requesting unit for approval/revision (2 minutes).","Administrative Officer, PACS – CSD contacts the requesting unit to receive the finalized job request (5 minutes).","Client receives the finished job order from PACS – CSD and accomplishes the Client Satisfaction Survey (CSS).","Administrative Officer, PACS – CSD administers the CSS (5 minutes)."]

internal_services.label: Provision of Photo and/or Video Services
internal_services.description: The process begins with receiving a request/directive for photo and/or video services, followed by planning and preparation for the shoot. After capturing the media, the editing phase occurs, where the best shots are selected and enhanced. Once editing is complete, the final digital copies are submitted to the requesting party. Additionally, all records of the request, production details, and final outputs are retained in the PACS – CSD database for future reference and compliance.
internal_services.whoMayAvail: All DILG – Central Office Operating Units
internal_services.office: Public Affairs and Communication Service – Creative Services Division (PACS – CSD)
internal_services.requirements: [{"text":"One (1) original or electronic copy of Provision of Photo and/or Video Services Request Tracker","where":"Client"}]
internal_services.steps: ["Send a directive or submit the Request Tracker via the Document Management System (DMS)/e-mail/Google Form.","Action Officer, PACS – CSD receives and records the request (5 minutes).","Section Chief/Division Chief, PACS – CSD evaluates and acts on the request (15 minutes).","Photographer/Videographer, PACS – CSD performs the requested service (2 working days, 7 hours, and 35 minutes).","Action Officer, PACS – CSD provides the Client Satisfaction Survey (CSS) to the requesting party for accomplishment (5 minutes).","Client accomplishes the Client Satisfaction Survey (CSS)."]

external_services.label: Issuance of Certificate for Foreign Travel Authority (FTA)
external_services.description: Local Government Officials and Employees must secure authority from the DILG Secretary before traveling abroad. The process supports online filing through the FTA Online System or filing through DILG offices.
external_services.requirements: [{"text":"Accomplished Citizen's Charter Request Form","where":"DILG Office"},{"text":"Online application through FTA system or via DILG email/DMS","where":"Client"},{"text":"Endorsement from appropriate authority (Mayor, Vice Governor, Vice Mayor, or Punong Barangay, as applicable)","where":"Concerned Local Authority"},{"text":"Invitation letter from host country or sponsor","where":"Host/Sponsor"},{"text":"Acceptance letter from organizer/CHED/TESDA/LGA (for study trips)","where":"Organizer/Agency"},{"text":"Notarized affidavit on presence or absence of administrative/criminal case","where":"Client/Notary Public"},{"text":"Clearance from money and property accountabilities","where":"Concerned Office"},{"text":"Memorandum of Agreement / Memorandum of Understanding (if applicable)","where":"Client/Concerned Office"},{"text":"Additional requirements for personal trips (leave of absence and medical certificate)","where":"Client"}]
external_services.steps: ["Prepare and complete all documentary requirements.","Submit application through the FTA online system or to the concerned DILG office.","Wait for document verification, review, and endorsement.","Receive the issued authority/certificate or notice of action."]

external_services.label: Certification of Compliance to Full Disclosure Policy (FDP)
external_services.description: The FDP promotes transparency and accountability by requiring LGUs to disclose financial transactions publicly. Certification is issued for LGUs applying for loans, national programs, or other lawful purposes.
external_services.requirements: [{"text":"Accomplished Citizen's Charter Request Form","where":"DILG Office"},{"text":"Letter request from Local Chief Executive stating purpose","where":"Client"}]
external_services.steps: ["Submit request form and letter request.","DILG validates submitted documents and FDP compliance records.","Receive certification of compliance."]

external_services.label: Department Authority for LGUs to Purchase Vehicle
external_services.description: Ensures that LGU vehicle purchases comply with DILG Memorandum Circular 2022-105 and DBM Budget Circular 2022-1 before acquisition.
external_services.requirements: [{"text":"Accomplished Citizen's Charter Request Form","where":"DILG Office"},{"text":"Endorsement from DILG Provincial/HUC Director","where":"DILG Provincial/HUC Office"},{"text":"Letter request from Local Chief Executive indicating purpose and vehicle details","where":"Client"},{"text":"Certificate of Availability of Funds","where":"Local Budget/Accounting Office"},{"text":"Certified copy of ordinance approving purchase","where":"Sanggunian Office"},{"text":"Updated inventory of existing motor vehicles","where":"Client"}]
external_services.steps: ["Prepare and submit complete documentary requirements.","DILG evaluates compliance with governing issuances.","Receive department authority or notice for compliance gaps."]

external_services.label: Certification and Accreditation to Civil Society Organizations
external_services.description: Allows CSOs and NGOs to be accredited to collaborate with DILG programs, strengthening government-civil society partnerships.
external_services.requirements: [{"text":"Citizen's Charter Request Form","where":"DILG Office"},{"text":"Application Form","where":"DILG Office"},{"text":"Letter of Intent","where":"Client"},{"text":"Certification under oath","where":"Client/Notary Public"},{"text":"Organizational chart and data sheet of officers","where":"Client"},{"text":"Annual accomplishment report","where":"Client"},{"text":"Board resolution authorizing application","where":"Client"},{"text":"Certificate of Registration (SEC, CDA, DOLE, etc.)","where":"Issuing Agency"},{"text":"Audited financial statements for the last 3 years","where":"Client/Auditor"},{"text":"Sworn certification regarding no derogatory record","where":"Client/Notary Public"},{"text":"Certificate of good standing","where":"Concerned Agency"},{"text":"Certification of no derogatory record","where":"Concerned Agency"},{"text":"Certification from government agencies where CSO partnered","where":"Concerned Agencies"}]
external_services.steps: ["File application with all required accreditation documents.","DILG reviews legal, organizational, and compliance submissions.","Receive certification/accreditation result."]

external_services.label: Authorization to Utilize Confidential Funds of LGUs
external_services.description: LGUs may request authorization to use confidential funds for peace and order concerns when properly allocated in their annual budget.
external_services.requirements: [{"text":"Citizen's Charter Request Form","where":"DILG Office"},{"text":"Three-Year Peace and Order Public Safety Plan","where":"Client"},{"text":"Peace and Order Council Resolution approving POPS plan","where":"Peace and Order Council"},{"text":"Certification from PNP regarding peace and order situation","where":"PNP"},{"text":"Annual/Supplemental Appropriations Ordinance","where":"Sanggunian Office"},{"text":"Certification from Local Budget Officer","where":"Local Budget Office"},{"text":"Statement of itemized Program of Projects","where":"Client"},{"text":"Certification of Full Disclosure Policy compliance","where":"DILG/Client"},{"text":"Physical and financial plan for fund usage","where":"Client"}]
external_services.steps: ["Submit request with complete budget and peace-and-order documents.","DILG conducts policy and documentary review.","Receive authorization or notice for additional compliance."]

external_services.label: Assistance to Former Rebels (ECLIP Program)
external_services.description: The Enhanced Comprehensive Local Integration Program provides assistance for former rebels or extremists who return to lawful society, including livelihood, reintegration, and financial assistance.
external_services.requirements: [{"text":"JAPIC Certification","where":"Concerned Authority"},{"text":"Enrolment Form","where":"DILG ECLIP Office"},{"text":"DILG ECLIP Form 7","where":"DILG ECLIP Office"},{"text":"Individual Reintegration Plan (for livelihood)","where":"Client/DILG ECLIP Office"},{"text":"Firearms inspection and documentation (for firearm remuneration)","where":"PNP/Concerned Authority"},{"text":"DILG ECLIP Form 8","where":"DILG ECLIP Office"},{"text":"DILG ECLIP Form 9","where":"DILG ECLIP Office"},{"text":"DILG ECLIP Form 10","where":"DILG ECLIP Office"}]
external_services.steps: ["Enroll in ECLIP and submit required forms and certifications.","DILG validates documents and coordinates with partner agencies.","Receive approved assistance package/reintegration support."]

external_services.label: Barangay Officials Death Benefit Assistance (BODBA)
external_services.description: Provides financial assistance to beneficiaries of deceased barangay officials based on Executive Order 139.
external_services.whoMayAvail: Qualified Beneficiaries of Deceased Barangay Officials
external_services.requirements: [{"text":"Citizen's Charter Request Form","where":"DILG Office"},{"text":"Oath of Office","where":"Client"},{"text":"Certificate of Incumbency","where":"Concerned Office"},{"text":"Death Certificate","where":"PSA/LCR"},{"text":"Government ID of claimant","where":"Client"},{"text":"Marriage Certificate (if applicable)","where":"PSA/LCR"},{"text":"Birth Certificate (if applicable)","where":"PSA/LCR"},{"text":"Affidavit of disinterested parties (if applicable)","where":"Client/Notary Public"},{"text":"Waiver of rights for multiple beneficiaries (if applicable)","where":"Client/Notary Public"},{"text":"Affidavit of guardianship (if applicable)","where":"Client/Notary Public"},{"text":"Special Power of Attorney (if applicable)","where":"Client/Notary Public"}]
external_services.steps: ["Submit claim packet and supporting documents.","DILG verifies beneficiary entitlement and documentary completeness.","Receive assistance approval and release instructions."]

external_services.label: Certification of Service for CSC Eligibility
external_services.description: Provides certification for barangay or local officials to apply for Civil Service Eligibility based on years of government service.
external_services.requirements: [{"text":"Citizen's Charter Request Form","where":"DILG Office"},{"text":"Certified copy of Oath of Office","where":"Concerned Office"},{"text":"Certification of service rendered","where":"Concerned Office"},{"text":"Appointment resolution (for barangay secretary/treasurer)","where":"Sanggunian Office"},{"text":"Birth certificate and marriage certificate","where":"PSA/LCR"}]
external_services.steps: ["Submit request and required proofs of service.","DILG validates records and tenure details.","Receive certification of service."]

external_services.label: Request for Technical Assistance (Resource Person)
external_services.description: DILG provides resource persons for training, seminars, and workshops related to government programs.
external_services.steps: ["Submit formal request letter.","DILG evaluates schedule and technical requirements.","Receive confirmation and assigned resource person details."]

external_services.label: Rendition of Legal Opinion
external_services.description: Provides official interpretation of the Local Government Code and related laws when legal questions arise.
external_services.requirements: [{"text":"Letter request addressed to DILG Secretary or Legal Service","where":"Client"}]
external_services.steps: ["Submit request outlining legal issue and context.","Legal Service reviews applicable laws and records.","Receive written legal opinion."]

external_services.label: Implementation of Ombudsman / Sandiganbayan / COMELEC Decisions
external_services.description: DILG evaluates and implements decisions, orders, or resolutions issued by these institutions involving local officials.
external_services.steps: ["File certified decision/order with DILG.","DILG validates authenticity and implementation requirements.","Receive notice/action on implementation."]

external_services.label: Position Papers on Congressional Measures
external_services.description: DILG prepares comments or position papers regarding proposed legislation affecting local governance.
external_services.requirements: [{"text":"Request or referral from concerned office or authority","where":"Client/Referring Office"}]
external_services.steps: ["Submit referral/request on proposed measure.","DILG conducts policy and legal review.","Receive endorsed comments/position paper."]

external_services.label: Recruitment, Selection, and Promotion (1st & 2nd Level Positions)
external_services.description: Handles hiring and promotion of personnel following civil service rules.
external_services.requirements: [{"text":"Application documents required under Civil Service rules (varies per position)","where":"Client"}]
external_services.steps: ["Submit complete application documents.","DILG HR conducts screening and evaluation.","Receive notice of action/result."]

external_services.label: Public Assistance and Handling of Complaints
external_services.description: Handles citizen inquiries, complaints, or assistance requests regarding DILG services.
external_services.requirements: [{"text":"Complaint or request filed by the concerned citizen","where":"Client"}]
external_services.steps: ["File complaint/request with required details.","Concerned office reviews and processes the concern.","Receive feedback or resolution update."]

external_services.label: Disposal of Property through Donation or Transfer
external_services.description: Allows disposal of government property through donation or transfer without cost.
external_services.steps: ["Submit disposal request with inventory and justification.","DILG evaluates compliance and documentation.","Receive approval/action for transfer or donation."]

external_services.label: Disposal of Property through Public Bidding
external_services.description: Allows disposal of government property through competitive bidding.
external_services.steps: ["Submit request and complete bidding documentation.","DILG reviews and processes disposal through public bidding procedures.","Receive notice of approval/action."]

external_services.label: Certification of Incumbency of Local Officials
external_services.description: Provides certification verifying that a person currently holds a local government office.
external_services.requirements: [{"text":"Citizen's Charter Request Form","where":"DILG Office"},{"text":"Supporting documents verifying incumbency","where":"Client/Concerned Office"}]
external_services.steps: ["Submit request form with supporting documents.","DILG validates records and incumbency status.","Receive certification of incumbency."]

issuances.subject: Updated Roles and Responsibilities of the DILG-LG Sector Committee on Anti-Red Tape (CART) and Additional Guidelines in the Implementation of the Ease of Doing Business and Efficient Government Service Delivery (EODB-EGSD) Act of 2018 in the Department
issuances.coverage: All offices in the DILG-LG Sector providing internal and external services included in the Citizen's Charter
issuances.supersedes: DILG Circular No. 2022-027 and prior inconsistent issuances
issuances.approvingAuthority: ATTY. BENJAMIN C. ABALOS, JR., Secretary
issuances.highlights: ["Updates the composition and functions of the DILG-CART and requires Regional Office CART Sub-Committees.","Requires compliance with RA 11032, its IRR, and ARTA issuances including process reengineering, ARP/RNF submissions, and regulatory impact requirements.","Mandates yearly Citizen's Charter updates, posting of information billboards, and online publication of the Citizen's Charter Handbook.","Implements harmonized CSM through e-CSM and requires annual Client Satisfaction reporting.","Requires ARTA helpdesk mechanisms and prompt handling of complaints from PCC, CCB, and ARTA Legal Office.","Requires support for e-BOSS implementation, Report Card Survey coordination, and communication/dissemination activities."]
issuances.deadlines: [{"dueDate":"March 07","label":"Submit Annual Regulatory Plan (ARP)"},{"dueDate":"March 07","label":"Submit Zero Backlog Report"},{"dueDate":"March 31","label":"Regional Offices submit signed Certificate of Compliance (CoC)"},{"dueDate":"Last working day of April","label":"Submit Client Satisfaction Reports to ARTA"},{"dueDate":"Within 15 days from issuance","label":"Register and publish regulations through ONAR and newspaper of general circulation"}]

feedback_content.title: Feedback and Complaints Mechanism
feedback_content.sections: [{"heading":"How to send feedback?","paragraphs":["Accomplish the Client Satisfaction Survey form after receiving your requested service from our action officers.","For other concerns, you may send an e-mail at paccrecords@gmail.com or call the telephone number (02) 8925-0343."]},{"heading":"How feedback is processed?","paragraphs":["Feedback on our services are immediately attended to by the concerned action officer.","Other feedback and concerns are endorsed by the Public Assistance and Complaints Center (PACC) to the appropriate office. Upon receiving the reply from the concerned office/personnel, the client will be informed via e-mail/phone call/letter."]},{"heading":"How to file complaint?","paragraphs":["For walk-ins at the Central Office: Accomplish the Client's Complaint Form available at the DILG Helpdesk at the Ground Floor, DILG-NAPOLCOM Center, EDSA cor. Quezon Avenue, West Triangle, Quezon City (Central Office).","For walk-ins at the Regional, Provincial, and Field Offices: Approach the Desk Officer of the Day and accomplish the Client's Complaint Form.","Online: Send an e-mail to paccrecords@gmail.com and provide the required details below."],"items":["Name (optional) and contact number of the complainant","Narrative/details of the complaint","Name of the office/LGU and/or official being complained"]},{"heading":"How complaints are processed?","paragraphs":["All complaints received will be reviewed by the PACC or the Desk Officer of the Day and endorsed to the concerned office for a reply or an appropriate action.","The PACC or the DILG Regional Office will provide feedback to the complainant via e-mail/letter."]}]

organizational_profile.title: Mandate, Mission, Vision and Service Pledge
organizational_profile.mandate: To promote peace and order, ensure public safety and further strengthen local government capability aimed towards the effective delivery of basic services to the citizenry.
organizational_profile.mission: The Department shall ensure peace and order, public safety and security, uphold excellence in local governance and enable resilient and inclusive communities.
organizational_profile.vision: A highly trusted Department and Partner in nurturing local governments and sustaining peaceful, safe, progressive, resilient, and inclusive communities towards a comfortable and secure life for Filipinos by 2040.
organizational_profile.pledgeIntro: We in the DILG, imbued with the core values of Integrity, Commitment, Teamwork and Responsiveness, commit to formulate sound policies on strengthening local government capacities, performing oversight function over LGUs, and providing rewards and incentives.
organizational_profile.pledgeServiceCommitment: We pledge to provide effective technical and administrative services through professionalized corps of civil servants to promote excellence in local governance specifically in the areas of PBEST:
organizational_profile.pbest: ["Peace and Order","Business-Friendliness and Competitiveness","Environment-Protection and Climate Change Adaptation","Socially Protective and Safe Communities","Transparency and Accountability"]
organizational_profile.pledgeOfficeHours: We commit to attend to clients who are within the premises of the office prior to the end of official working hours and during lunch break.
organizational_profile.pledgeClosing: We commit to consistently demonstrate a "Matino, Mahusay at Maaasahang Kagawaran para sa Mapagkalinga at Maunlad na Pamahalaang Lokal".
